methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Aeon Industrial is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $18.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public peers operating in the same sector and stage window, including Series A companies Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M), as well as Seed-stage peer Tenna Systems ($60M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($19M) as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implies a $46M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the four comparable companies yields a $225M implied valuation, with the four-company set ranging from $60M to $500M. The disparity between these methods reflects the wide performance spread within the Defense & Security sector at this stage.

Synthesizing both approaches produces a triangulated range of $40M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $136M. The wide spread—driven primarily by the peer-median method's sensitivity to high-performing outliers like Stark Defence—indicates material uncertainty around where Aeon Industrial fits within its peer cohort. Key variables affecting valuation include product maturity, customer traction, and defensibility relative to established competitors in the sector.
